_comment: Per-agent gate profiles (D2a, 2026-07-13 weight audit). Pick a profile at install time and wire only its groups into <agent-wd>/.claude/settings.json. Gate FILES stay in this repo (single source of truth) — profiles select WIRINGS, never copy gate code or rules text. NOTE: Claude Code project settings are cwd-scoped and do NOT merge parent directories — the agent must LAUNCH with cwd == the directory holding .claude/settings.json.

safety-core.description: Content-triggered verification gates (classification B) — self-scoping, silent-pass cost is tens of ms; measured 70-100% true-positive on live blocks. Recommended for every interactive agent.

workflow.description: Owner-contract workflow gates. prompt-advance bounces execution-grade starts once when a crystallized task skipped the prompt-engineering pass (narrowed 2026-07-13: meeting-driven sessions exempt, small edits exempt).

opt-in.description: Explicit opt-in gates (cutover-review, requirements-lock, branch-stray-guard) — wire only when the owner opts in.

orchestrator.notes: Dispatch verification and delegate-report discipline matter most here (subordinate-evidence measured 4/4 true-positive). Workflow gate usually redundant: orchestrated tasks arrive already spec'd.

generalist.notes: Do-everything agents benefit from the full set — our audit found the generalist bot had ZERO gates wired, which was the gap, not the weight.

research.notes: claim-evidence and absence gates align directly with research discipline (unverified counts, absence claims).

writing.pilot: claim-evidence: run 1 week in observation mode — manuscript numbers may read as count claims (unverified hypothesis; bounded by MAX_CLAIM_BLOCKS=1/session).
writing.notes: Persona/format gates for writing are platform hooks (classification A), not tofable gates.

schedule-automation.notes: Wire ONLY after ticker/cron sessions carry FABLE_GATE_OFF=1 in their launch env — otherwise stop-gates bounce headless beats. Order matters.

pipeline.notes: blind-retry and subordinate-evidence pay off most (retry loops, multi-stage delegation).

visual.notes: Image-safety guards (text-overlay blocking etc.) are platform content-hooks — keep them global on the platform side, not per-bot.

codex.notes: Use codex/gates/ (shared ledger schema) — same safety core, platform adapters differ (reply id-dedupe, turn-boundary diff). Do not fork rule text per platform.

constraints: Never copy gate code or rules text per agent — profiles select wirings; the repo stays the single source of truth (drift risk is the #1 failure mode of per-agent differentiation).

automation-carveout-first: headless/cron launch env needs FABLE_GATE_OFF=1 (tofable gates). If your platform hooks have their own automation flag, set both — env names are layer-specific and unimplemented names fail silently (measured).
Launch cwd must equal the settings directory (cwd-scoped, no parent merge — measured with a controlled probe).
Pilot per agent for a week (FABLE_GATE_PILOT), re-run the audit scanner on that agent's transcripts, then go fleet-wide.
